title: client_id=1 hardcoded; no URI param to configure it
description: Client::connect(transport, 1) hardcodes client_id=1. wolfHSM namespaces NVM objects and keys by client_id. If two secretx backends connect to the same wolfHSM server with different labels, they share client_id=1 and their NVM namespaces collide — intended behaviour, since they're two halves of the same application. But if two independent applications both connect to the same wolfHSM server, they cannot avoid colliding. The maintenance consequence: users will file issues about label collisions with no clue that client_id is the cause. Fix: add optional ?client_id=N URI param, defaulting to 1.
